page: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a member of the Dldsv7 server family, utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture. This virtualized instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features 64 dedicated vCPUs. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

The server includes 3780 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-speed disk operations. It does not include integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware configuration represents a low-memory profile relative to the high vCPU count, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. This resource balance provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require substantial processing power but have modest memory footprints.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include compute-heavy tasks, batch processing, web serving, and database operations that benefit from large local storage capacity without requiring massive memory allocations.
description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dldsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance is equipped with 3780 GB of local storage and supports premium storage options, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This low-memory, high-compute configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for processing-intensive applications that do not demand extensive RAM. It is suited for batch processing, web serving, and storage-reliant database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a Dldsv7 family virtual server featuring 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and storage-reliant workloads.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, low memory density, and substantial local storage capacity for processing-intensive workloads.
